tags.name: Black Forest
tags.qa_notes: Difficulty upgraded Intermediate→Advanced: SM Divers explicitly states 'requires more experience due to strong currents and excellent buoyancy control required.' Alona Divers rates Intermediate-Advanced. Black coral forest starts 35-45m (not 25m). Marine life: jackfish schools, blackfin barracuda, trevallies, anthias, wrasses, sea turtles, scorpionfish, groupers confirmed across multiple operator sources. Hammerhead/manta NOT confirmed at this site — removed from description. validation_source updated from single domain to three independent sources.

tags.name: Marine Sanctuary
tags.qa_notes: Depth corrected 30m→35m: Alona Divers Club lists site 3-35m, SM Divers lists 5-25m in one source but wall overhang goes deeper. Multiple sources confirm turtles, jackfish, barracuda, surgeonfish, lionfish, triggerfish. Beginner maintained: calm protected zone. validation_source updated to three independent sources.

tags.name: Pamilacan Island
tags.qa_notes: Difficulty upgraded Intermediate→Advanced: Multiple sources (SM Divers, Valm Divers, alonadivers.com) recommend Advanced Open Water certification minimum due to moderate-to-strong currents. Dakit-Dakit exposed with strong drift currents. Manta ray/whale shark claims stripped — sources say 'occasional at Spanish Tower' only, not confirmed across all Pamilacan sites. validation_source updated from single domain to three independent sources.

tags.name: The Arc
tags.qa_notes: UNRESOLVED: No independent source identifies a site named 'The Arc' separately from 'Arco Point' in Panglao. Coordinates (9.55, 123.77) are nearly identical to Arco Point (9.555, 123.775) and consistent with the BBC/Bohol Beach Club area. The site may be a duplicate of Arco Point or the BBC site. Difficulty changed Intermediate→Beginner based on BBC site characteristics (all-levels, artificial wrecks, calm conditions). Cannot validate with 3 independent sources under this name. Marked unresolved pending local operator confirmation.

tags.name: Doljo Point
tags.qa_notes: Difficulty upgraded Intermediate→Advanced: SM Divers requires 'Advanced Open Water certification minimum; excellent buoyancy control mandatory.' Wall drops to 60m+. Depth corrected from 30m to 40m (recreational limit on a 60m+ wall). Hammerhead sharks and manta rays removed — not confirmed at this site in any source reviewed. Marine life confirmed: giant frogfish in barrel sponges, gorgonian sea fans up to car-door size, nudibranchs, ghost pipefish, surgeonfish, batfish, sweetlips, trevally, dogtooth tuna, eagle rays, reef sharks, chevron barracuda. validation_source upgraded from 'gap_fill' to three independent sources.

tags.name: Kalipayan Wall
tags.qa_notes: Difficulty changed Intermediate→Beginner: Tropical Divers lists 'Beginner-Intermediate', Valm Divers lists 'Moderate', house reef accessible to beginners with calm conditions and no significant current. Wall base at ~20m, depth tag 30m refers to SSI listing of outer area. Marine life confirmed across three operators: batfish, lionfish, nudibranchs, ghost pipefish, dragon seamoth, seahorses, soft corals. Stonefish mentioned in earlier markdown not confirmed by 3 sources — described as 'reported' rather than confirmed. validation_source upgraded from 'gap_fill' to three independent sources.

tags.name: Arco Point
tags.qa_notes: Swim-through direction corrected: entrance at ~8-9m shallow, exits deeper at ~18m (previous description had this reversed). SM Divers and Alona Divers confirm entrance is the shallow end, exit is the deeper end. Marine life confirmed across three sources: frogfish (painted and warty), banded sea kraits, nudibranchs (Chromodoris, Flabellina), moray eels (yellow-edged, snowflake, ribbon), sweeper fish, cardinal fish. Blue-ring octopus mentioned in some sources as possible sighting. Whale shark claim stripped — appears in one aggregator source only, not confirmed by specialist operator sites. validation_source upgraded from 'gap_fill' to three independent sources.

tags.name: Snake Island
tags.qa_notes: MAJOR CORRECTION — Difficulty upgraded Beginner→Advanced: SM Divers explicitly says 'not a site for absolute beginners' and 'advanced guests or experienced divers comfortable in drift conditions.' Valm Divers rates it Advanced. Strong sweeping surface currents. Site type changed reef→drift: it's a seamount/sunken plateau with strong currents, best characterized as a drift site. Plateau top at 12-15m, drops to 60m+. Thresher shark claim removed — thresher sharks are a Malapascua phenomenon (Kimud Shoal), not confirmed at Cervera Shoal. Marine life confirmed: black-and-white banded sea kraits (Laticauda colubrina), white-eyed morays, starry morays, nudibranchs, bucket sponges. validation_source upgraded from 'gap_fill' to three independent sources.
